questions.question_text: How many total CE hours must a Washington broker complete for the first license renewal?
questions.explanation: Washington requires 90 hours of continuing education for the first renewal: 30-hour Advanced Practices, 30-hour Real Estate Law, 3-hour Current Issues (WA Core), and 27 hours of elective courses.

questions.question_text: Before starting first-renewal CE courses, a new broker must first complete which course?
questions.explanation: New Washington brokers must complete a 6-hour initial Fair Housing course before beginning their other first-renewal continuing education hours. This is a one-time requirement for newly licensed brokers.

questions.question_text: A newly licensed broker asks what the '3-hour WA Core' course covers during first renewal. What is its focus?
questions.explanation: The 3-hour WA Core course focuses on current issues affecting real estate practice in Washington. It is also called the Current Issues course and is required at every renewal, including the first.

questions.question_text: A broker completed 60 of the 90 first-renewal CE hours. Which required course is most likely missing from her transcript?
questions.explanation: The first renewal requires 30-hour Advanced Practices, 30-hour RE Law, 3-hour WA Core, and 27 hours elective (totaling 90). If she completed 60 hours, she likely finished Advanced Practices and the electives but still needs the 30-hour Real Estate Law course.

questions.question_text: When do first-renewal CE hours begin accruing for a Washington broker?
questions.explanation: First-renewal CE hours start accruing after a broker receives their initial license. Pre-license education hours do not count toward the 90-hour first-renewal CE requirement.

questions.question_text: How many CE hours are required for each subsequent license renewal after the first?
questions.explanation: After the initial 90-hour first renewal, each subsequent renewal requires 30 hours of continuing education: 3-hour WA Core, 3-hour Fair Housing, and 24 hours of electives.

questions.question_text: How many hours of Fair Housing CE are required at each subsequent renewal?
questions.explanation: After completing the initial 6-hour Fair Housing course at first renewal, each subsequent renewal requires a 3-hour Fair Housing course. This ongoing requirement ensures licensees stay current on fair housing law.

questions.question_text: A broker completed 20 CE hours in year one and 18 hours in year two of a renewal cycle. How many excess hours may carry forward?
questions.explanation: Washington allows up to 15 excess CE hours to carry forward from within 48 months of the renewal date. While this broker earned 38 total hours (8 excess), the maximum carryover is capped at 15 hours regardless of how many extra were completed.

questions.question_text: How often must a Washington broker renew their license?
questions.explanation: Washington uses a biennial (every 2 years) renewal cycle. At least 15 of the required 30 CE hours must be completed within the 24 months immediately preceding renewal.

questions.question_text: A broker completed all 30 CE hours in the first month of her 2-year renewal cycle. Does this meet the timing requirement?
questions.correct_answer: No, 15 hours must be within 24 months
distractors: No, all must be in the final year
questions.explanation: Washington requires at least 15 of the 30 CE hours to be completed within the 24 months immediately preceding renewal. Completing all hours in month one would mean those hours fall outside the 24-month window by renewal time.

questions.question_text: A broker's license expired 60 days ago. What is the late renewal penalty?
questions.explanation: Washington allows late renewal within 120 days of expiration with a $40 penalty added to the standard renewal fee. After 120 days, the licensee must apply for reinstatement instead.

questions.question_text: How many days after license expiration can a Washington broker still use the late renewal process?
questions.explanation: Washington provides a 120-day grace period for late renewal with a $40 penalty. After 120 days, the licensee must go through the reinstatement process, which costs $100 plus the renewal fee.

questions.question_text: A broker's license expired 150 days ago. What must she pay to restore her license?
questions.explanation: Since the license expired more than 120 days ago, the late renewal option is no longer available. The broker must apply for reinstatement, which costs $100 plus the standard renewal fee.

questions.question_text: A broker is transferring from one firm to another. What step must be completed?
questions.correct_answer: Notify the DOL of the transfer
questions.explanation: When a broker transfers between firms, the DOL must be notified. The broker keeps the same license and license number but must be affiliated with a firm to actively practice real estate.

questions.question_text: A broker left her firm last month and has not yet joined another. Can she continue practicing real estate?
questions.correct_answer: No, must be with a firm to practice
questions.explanation: Washington requires brokers to be affiliated with a firm to actively practice real estate. A broker who is between firms cannot conduct real estate business until they affiliate with a new firm and notify the DOL.
